title: Add a host-local socket or named-pipe guard beside the durable file lock.
summary: loop-cli-main binds local IPC before state initialization, so a losing same-host starter fails before loop state loads. OUR lock layer should expose this as an optional host-local guard, not as a replacement for the file lock.
whyItHelps: Gives same-host deep-loop starts a kernel-enforced admission check before reducers and state logs initialize while preserving the durable packet-level claim.

title: Socket single-flight only works if the holder process stays alive.
summary: loop-cli-main keeps the net.Server open until daemon shutdown. OUR current loop-lock CLI acquire path exits immediately after writing JSON, so a socket opened there would be dropped before the workflow runs.
whyItHelps: Prevents false hardening: a one-shot helper cannot own a kernel-bound socket lease for a long-running research or review loop.

title: Socket binding is local single-flight, not multi-host packet fencing.
summary: loop-cli-main explicitly uses local socket or pipe IPC with no network listener. For OUR shared spec packets, two machines can each own a different local IPC namespace, so lock metadata should distinguish host-local guard strength from true shared lease enforcement.
whyItHelps: Keeps status and documentation honest: socket bind hardens same-host startup races, but multi-host writes need a separate shared lease or remain best-effort.

title: Probe an existing socket before removing it.
summary: loop-cli-main unlinks the socket path before binding. If OUR runtime ports the idea, it should add a stale-socket probe first rather than blindly copying remove-then-bind.
whyItHelps: Avoids erasing the pathname for a still-live local listener and gives stale cleanup a concrete owner/probe decision.

summary: OUR current lock does not use flock directly; it uses exclusive file creation for fresh acquire and rename plus exclusive create for stale reclaim. The YAML advisory wording should be tightened around filesystem and host-boundary limits rather than implying the code is only an advisory flock.
S3-01 already covered heartbeat refresh and the unwired refresh CLI; this iteration adds the separate local socket-guard, lifetime, stale-socket cleanup, and multi-host boundary mapping.
